features.2: Sincronizza facilmente i tuoi TOTP (*Time-based One-Time Password*).

1.title: Formato standard
1.description: Compatibile con tutti i servizi che supportano l’autenticazione a due fattori: Google, GitHub, Discord e migliaia di altri.

2.title: Sincronizzazione
2.description: Sarebbe un peccato perdere l’accesso ai tuoi account quando cambi telefono. Puoi anche ospitare autonomamente il backend se vuoi ([maggiori informazioni](https://github.com/openauthenticator-app/backend)).

3.title: Dati privati
3.description: I tuoi dati vengono cifrati prima della trasmissione usando una chiave derivata da una password principale.

4.title: Multipiattaforma
4.description: Un solo progetto, tutte le piattaforme. Android, iOS, macOS, Windows e Linux — realizzato con Flutter.

5.title: Traduzioni
5.description: L’app è multilingue ed è tradotta dalla community. Unisciti ai collaboratori e aiutaci a renderla accessibile a tutti.

6.title: Ultraleggera
6.description: Nessun eccesso, nessuna autorizzazione inutile. L’app fa ciò che promette, e niente altro, rapidamente.

title.content:
Tutto ciò che serve.
*Niente di più.*

features.description: Progettata per essere semplice, affidabile e rispettosa dei tuoi dati. Senza compromessi sulla sicurezza.

download.description: Realizzato con Flutter, Open Authenticator funziona in modo nativo su tutte le principali piattaforme. Scegli la tua.

description.1: Il codice sorgente è completamente pubblico sotto licenza GNU GPL v3. Puoi verificarlo, crearne un fork, contribuire o semplicemente fidarti.
description.2: Vuoi aiutare? Contribuisci con pull request su GitHub, suggerisci nuove traduzioni o fai una donazione per sostenere il progetto.

getStarted.description: Gratuita, open-source, multipiattaforma e facile da usare. Nessuna pubblicità, nessun tracciamento, nessuna raccolta di dati. Scarica subito Open Authenticator e riprendi il controllo della tua sicurezza.

faq.description: Tutto ciò che devi sapere su Open Authenticator — sicurezza, privacy e altro.

1.question: Cosa devo fare se dimentico la mia password principale?
1.answer:
Non possiamo fare nulla. Davvero. I tuoi TOTP sono cifrati usando una chiave derivata dalla tua password principale con l’algoritmo [Argon2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Argon2). Non potremo recuperare i tuoi dati se dimentichi la password principale. Per questo motivo consigliamo vivamente di effettuare backup regolari.

L’app ti chiede automaticamente di fare un backup prima di ogni operazione sensibile, ma puoi anche crearne uno manualmente nelle impostazioni dell’app.

2.question: Dove vengono archiviati i miei dati?
2.answer:
Se non hai abilitato la sincronizzazione dei dati, tutto viene archiviato localmente in un database SQLite gestito con [Drift](https://drift.simonbinder.eu/). Anche in quel caso, tutto è cifrato.

Se hai abilitato la sincronizzazione dei dati, tutto viene archiviato in forma non cifrata sul nostro backend, il cui codice sorgente è disponibile [qui](https://github.com/openauthenticator-app/backend).

3.question: Cos’è l’Abbonamento Contributor?
3.answer:
Come forse sai, i server non sono gratuiti. Nel nostro caso, più utenti abbiamo, più aumentano i costi. Quindi, queste sono le opzioni disponibili.

1. Inseriamo annunci nell’app.
2. Ci affidiamo esclusivamente alle donazioni.
3. Chiediamo agli utenti di contribuire ai costi operativi.

L’opzione 2 non è affatto realistica. L’opzione 1 potrebbe essere una soluzione, ma gli annunci di solito peggiorano l’esperienza utente e comportano tracker. Pertanto, la scelta fatta per coprire i costi è creare un modello di abbonamento chiamato *Abbonamento Contributor*. Attualmente puoi archiviare e sincronizzare gratuitamente fino a sei TOTP su tutti i tuoi dispositivi. Abbonandoti all’Abbonamento Contributor, potrai sincronizzare tutti i TOTP che vuoi.

In futuro speriamo di allentare queste limitazioni, o persino rimuoverle del tutto. In ogni caso, puoi usare l’app localmente senza limitazioni e senza annunci.

4.question: Devo scaricarla anche se il mio gestore di password supporta i TOTP?
4.answer:
Sì, dovresti. Se il tuo gestore di password venisse compromesso, sia le tue password *sia* i tuoi TOTP sarebbero esposti nello stesso momento. Di conseguenza, il secondo fattore di autenticazione non servirebbe più al suo scopo.

*Non mettere tutte le uova nello stesso paniere!*

5.question: Non riesco ad abilitare l’autenticazione locale su Linux. Cosa devo fare?
5.answer:
Se questo è il tuo caso, probabilmente significa che Open Authenticator è stato installato tramite Flatpak.

L’autenticazione locale su Linux si basa su un file di policy Polkit che deve essere presente nel sistema host in `/usr/share/polkit-1/actions`.

Se non è presente, puoi installarlo copiando [questo file](https://flatpak.openauthenticator.app/app.openauthenticator.policy) in `/usr/share/polkit-1/actions`. Poi riavvia Open Authenticator (o la tua sessione).

6.question: È possibile ospitare autonomamente il backend?
6.answer: Sì. Dalla versione v2.0.0 dell’app puoi ospitare il backend sul tuo server. Le istruzioni sono disponibili nella [pagina GitHub del backend](https://github.com/openauthenticator-app/backend) (in inglese).

questionLeft.description: Un argomento non è trattato qui? Non esitare a chiederci qualsiasi cosa. Ti risponderemo il prima possibile.

privacyPolicy.intro: This privacy policy is applicable to the Open Authenticator app (hereinafter referred to as "Application") for mobile devices, which was developed by Skyost (hereinafter referred to as "Service Provider") as a subscription supported service. This service is provided "AS IS".

userProvidedInfo.title: User Provided Information
userProvidedInfo.content:
The Application acquires the information you supply when you download, configure, register, log in to,
or use the Application. Registration with the Service Provider is not mandatory for local-only use.
However, you must register or log in to use synchronization, provider linking, account deletion, and
subscription-related features. Depending on the login method you choose, the Service Provider may process
your email address or the identifier returned by your authentication provider (Google, Apple, GitHub, or
Microsoft), as well as a backend user identifier, linked providers, your subscription status, and a stable
Application installation identifier used to bind sessions to your device. If you use email login, the
Service Provider processes your email address and temporary verification, cancellation, and authorization
codes in order to complete the login flow.

automaticallyCollectedInfo.title: Automatically Collected Information
automaticallyCollectedInfo.content:
In addition, the Application and its backend may collect certain information automatically, including,
but not limited to, the type of device you use, the stable Application installation identifier generated
by the Application, your IP address, your operating system, your app version, your user agent, request
headers needed to operate the backend, and information about the way you use the Application.
This information is used to provide the service, secure sessions, enforce rate limits, maintain
compatibility, and troubleshoot issues.
The Application and backend also send diagnostic information to a private GlitchTip instance, using the
Sentry-compatible error reporting protocol, when bugs or crashes occur. These reports may include
technical details such as the app version, operating system version, device model, error message,
stack trace, timestamp, recent actions in the Application, and, for backend errors, request information
such as the HTTP method, URL, and request headers. Your master password and TOTP secrets are not
intentionally sent in these reports.

locationInfo.title: Does the Application collect precise real-time location information of the device ?
locationInfo.content:
This Application does not gather precise information about the location of your device.
The Application may request camera access only to scan TOTP QR codes. Camera images are processed on
your device for scanning and are not intentionally sent to the Service Provider.
If you enable biometric or local authentication, authentication is handled by your device operating system;
the Application does not receive or transmit your biometric data.

thirdPartyAccess.title: Do third parties see and/or have access to information obtained by the Application ?
thirdPartyAccess.content:
Aggregated, anonymized data may be periodically transmitted to internal services to aid the
Service Provider in improving the Application and their service. Diagnostic information related to bugs
or crashes may be transmitted to the Service Provider's private GlitchTip instance for error monitoring
and troubleshooting. Data needed to operate the service may be processed by infrastructure providers,
authentication providers, RevenueCat, app stores, and optional logo search services when you use the
corresponding features. The Service Provider may share your information with third parties in the ways
that are described in this privacy statement.

thirdPartyProviders.title: Third-Party Providers
thirdPartyProviders.content:
Please note that the Application utilizes third-party services that have their own
Privacy Policy about handling data. Below are the links to the Privacy Policy of the
third-party service providers used by the Application :

disclosure.title: Disclosure of Information
disclosure.content: The Service Provider may disclose User Provided and Automatically Collected Information :

list.1: as required by law, such as to comply with a subpoena, or similar legal process;
list.2: when they believe in good faith that disclosure is necessary to protect their rights, protect your safety or the safety of others, investigate fraud, or respond to a government request;
list.3: with their trusted services providers who work on their behalf, do not have an independent use of the information we disclose to them, and have agreed to adhere to the rules set forth in this privacy statement.

optOut.title: What are my opt-out rights?
optOut.content:
You can halt all collection of information by the Application easily by uninstalling the
Application. You may use the standard uninstall processes as may be available as part of your mobile device
or via the mobile application marketplace or network.

dataRetention.title: Data Retention Policy, Managing Your Information
dataRetention.content:
The Service Provider will retain User Provided data for as long as you use the
Application and for a reasonable time thereafter. Backend refresh sessions are normally retained until
they expire or are revoked. Deleted TOTP synchronization markers and processed RevenueCat webhook event
records may be retained for up to 365 days before pruning. Inactive non-contributor accounts without
active sessions or stored TOTPs may be pruned.
If you delete your account from the Application, the Service Provider deletes the backend user record,
backend sessions, and synchronized TOTP data controlled by the Service Provider. Data held by third-party
providers, app stores, or RevenueCat remains subject to their own retention and account-management rules.
The Service Provider will retain Automatically Collected information for up to 24 months and thereafter
may store it in aggregate.
If you'd like the Service Provider to delete User Provided Data that you have provided via the Application,
please [contact them](/contact/) and they will respond in a reasonable time.
Please note that some or all of the User Provided Data may be required in order for the Application
to function properly.
Your master password is not transmitted to any remote server.
**If you forget it, we cannot help you recover it.**
Please be aware that, although we endeavor to provide reasonable security for
information we process and maintain, no security system can prevent all potential security breaches.

children.title: Children
children.content:
The Service Provider does not use the Application to knowingly solicit data from or market
to children under the age of 13. The Application does not address anyone under the age of 13.
The Service Provider does not knowingly collect personally identifiable information from children under
13 years of age.

security.title: Security
security.content:
The Service Provider is concerned about safeguarding the confidentiality of your information.
The Service Provider provides physical, electronic, and procedural safeguards to protect information we
process and maintain. If you choose to synchronize your data between your devices, the backend stores
your TOTP records in encrypted form. The TOTP secret, label, issuer, and image URL are encrypted using
an [AES-GCM](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Galois/Counter_Mode) algorithm with an
[Argon2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Argon2) derived key based on your master password and a random salt.
Some synchronization metadata, such as the TOTP UUID, algorithm, digit count, validity period, update
time, and deletion markers, may be stored without being encrypted by the master password so synchronization
can work correctly.

changes.title: Changes
changes.content:
This Privacy Policy may be updated from time to time for any reason.
The Service Provider will notify you of any changes to the Privacy Policy by updating this page with the new
Privacy Policy.
changes.effectiveDate: This privacy policy is effective as of April 1, 2024.

contact.title: Contact Us
contact.content:
If you have any questions regarding privacy while using the Application, or
have questions about the practices, please [contact](/contact/) the Service Provider.

privacyPolicy.credit: Thanks to [nisrulz](https://app-privacy-policy-generator.firebaseapp.com/) for this privacy policy.

termsOfService.intro:
These terms and conditions applies to the Open Authenticator app (hereby referred to as "Application")
for mobile devices that was created by Skyost (hereby referred to as "Service Provider") as a subscription supported service.
termsOfService.agreement:
Upon downloading or utilizing the Application, you are automatically agreeing to the following terms
and to the [Privacy policy](/privacy-policy).
It is strongly advised that you thoroughly read and understand these terms prior to using the Application.
Unauthorized copying, modification of the Application, any part of the Application, or our trademarks
is strictly prohibited.
Any attempts to create derivative versions or "copycats" are not permitted.
All trademarks, copyrights, database rights, and other intellectual property rights related to the
Application remain the property of the Service Provider.
termsOfService.modification:
The Service Provider is dedicated to ensuring that the Application is as beneficial and efficient as
possible. As such, they reserve the right to modify the Application or charge for their services at
any time and for any reason. The Service Provider assures you that any charges for the Application or
its services will be clearly communicated to you.
termsOfService.dataProcessing:
The Application stores and processes personal data that you have provided to the Service Provider in
order to provide the Service, including account, authentication, synchronization, diagnostic, and
subscription-related data as described in the Privacy Policy. It is your responsibility to maintain the
security of your device and access to the Application. The Service Provider strongly advises against
jailbreaking or rooting your phone, which involves removing software restrictions and limitations imposed
by the official operating system of your device. Such actions could expose your phone to malware, viruses,
malicious programs, compromise your phone's security features, and may result in the Application not
functioning correctly or at all.
termsOfService.thirdPartyTerms:
Please note that the Application utilizes third-party services that have their own Terms and Conditions.
Below are the links to the Terms and Conditions of the third-party service providers used by the
Application :
termsOfService.responsibility:
Please be aware that the Service Provider does not assume responsibility for certain aspects.
Some functions of the Application require an active internet connection, which can be Wi-Fi or provided
by your mobile network provider. The Service Provider cannot be held responsible if the Application does
not function at full capacity due to lack of access to Wi-Fi or if you have exhausted your data allowance.
termsOfService.charges:
If you are using the application outside of a Wi-Fi area, please be aware that your mobile network
provider's agreement terms still apply. Consequently, you may incur charges from your mobile provider
for data usage during the connection to the application, or other third-party charges.
By using the application, you accept responsibility for any such charges, including roaming data
charges if you use the application outside of your home territory (i.e., region or country) without
disabling data roaming.
The Contributor Plan is handled through the applicable app store, RevenueCat, or RevenueCat Web Billing,
depending on your platform. Subscriptions may renew automatically and must be managed or cancelled
through the platform or billing provider that processed the purchase. Refunds, trials, renewals, and
cancellations are subject to the rules of the applicable platform and to mandatory consumer protection laws.
If you are not the bill payer for the device on which you are using the application, they assume
that you have obtained permission from the bill payer.
termsOfService.battery:
Similarly, the Service Provider cannot always assume responsibility for your usage of the application.
For instance, it is your responsibility to ensure that your device remains charged.
If your device runs out of battery and you are unable to access the Service, the Service Provider
cannot be held responsible.
termsOfService.termination:
The Service Provider may wish to update the application at some point.
The application is currently available as per the requirements for the operating system
(and for any additional systems they decide to extend the availability of the application to) may change,
and you will need to download the updates if you want to continue using the application.
The Service Provider does not guarantee that it will always update the application so that it is relevant
to you and/or compatible with the particular operating system version installed on your device.
However, you agree to always accept updates to the application when offered to you.
The Service Provider may also wish to cease providing the application and may terminate its use at any
time without providing termination notice to you. Unless they inform you otherwise, upon any termination,
(a) the rights and licenses granted to you in these terms will end; (b) you must cease using the application,
and (if necessary) delete it from your device.

noWarranty.title: No warranty
noWarranty.content:
All Content (including but not limited to your TOTPs) is made available AS IS and the Service Provider
does not offer any warranty of any kind, or represent that the Content will be accurate, complete,
or error-free.

synchronizationSecurity.title: Synchronization and security
synchronizationSecurity.content:
If you choose to synchronize your Data between your devices, you acknowledge that encrypted TOTP data,
synchronization metadata, account identifiers, authentication-provider identifiers, sessions, and
subscription status may be stored on the Service Provider's backend and infrastructure providers.
Legacy Firebase data may be processed if you use the migration feature from an older version of the
Application. Please be aware that, although we endeavor to provide reasonable security for information we
process and maintain, no security system can prevent all potential security breaches.
Therefore, we are not liable for any data loss, any leak, or any damage resulting from the use of
the application.

releaseIndemnity.title: Release and indemnity
releaseIndemnity.content:
To the extent permitted by applicable law, you agree to release and waive any and all
claims and/or liability against the Service Provider arising from connection with your use of the
Application or any subscription to the Application. You also agree to defend, indemnify and hold harmless
the Service Provider, officers, directors, employees, partners, contributors, or licensors from and
against any and all claims, damages, obligations, losses, liabilities, (including but not limited to
attorney's fees) arising from : (i) your use of and access to the Application; (ii) your violation of any
term of these terms of use; and (iii) your violation of any third party right, including without limitation
any copyright, property, or privacy right.

limitationOfLiability.title: Limitation of Liability
limitationOfLiability.content:
As stated before, under no circonstances, including negligence, shall the Service Provider, officers, directors,
employees, partners, contributors, or licensors be liable for any direct, indirect, incidental, special,
punitive or consequential damages that may result from the access of, use or inability to use the Application content,
including without limitation, use of or reliance on information, interruptions, errors, defects, mistakes,
omissions, deletions of files, delays in operations or transmission, non-delivery of information, disclosure of
communications, or any other failure of performance.

changes.title: Changes to These Terms and Conditions
changes.content:
The Service Provider may periodically update their Terms and Conditions.
Therefore, you are advised to review this page regularly for any changes.
The Service Provider will notify you of any changes by posting the new Terms and Conditions on this page.
changes.effectiveDate: These terms and conditions are effective as of 2024-04-01.

contact.title: Contact Us
contact.content:
If you have any questions or suggestions about the Terms and Conditions,
please do not hesitate to [contact](/contact/) the Service Provider.

termsOfService.credit: Thanks to [nisrulz](https://app-privacy-policy-generator.firebaseapp.com/) for these terms of service.

contact.description: Per tutto ciò che riguarda lo sviluppo di Open Authenticator — bug, suggerimenti, contributi — apri direttamente una [issue GitHub](https://github.com/openauthenticator-app/openauthenticator). Per qualsiasi altra richiesta (eliminazione account, domanda generale, ecc.), usa il modulo qui sotto.

form.success: La tua richiesta è stata inviata con successo.
form.error: Si è verificato un errore durante l’invio della richiesta.

translate.description: Parli un’altra lingua? Aiuta a rendere l’app accessibile a tutti. Scegli una lingua qui sotto per iniziare. Se la tua non è disponibile, [inviaci un messaggio](/contact/) e la aggiungeremo.
translate.languageDescription:
La traduzione è organizzata in più file. Per tradurne uno, devi prima espanderlo.
Una volta fatto, vedrai i testi originali nella colonna di sinistra e la traduzione esistente a destra.
Quando hai finito di tradurre un file, puoi inviarlo cliccando sul pulsante verde di invio.

Puoi salvare i progressi in qualsiasi momento usando il pulsante *Salva*.
Il pulsante *Carica* ti permetterà poi di riprendere la traduzione.

modal.title: Grazie per *contribuire* a Open Authenticator!
modal.subtitle: Segui i passaggi qui sotto per inviare la tua traduzione.

grabCode.title: Copia il codice
grabCode.copyCode: Per prima cosa, devi copiare il codice qui sotto.

openGithub.title: Apri Github
openGithub.click: Clicca sul link seguente per modificare il file corrente su Github.
openGithub.note:
**Nota.** Hai bisogno di un account Github.
Per favore [creane uno](https://github.com/) prima di proseguire.

fork.title: Crea un fork del repository
fork.pasteTheCode: Fatto ciò, incolla il codice copiato durante il *Passaggio **#1***.
fork.commit: Poi clicca su **Commit changes...** e su **Propose changes**.

pullRequest.title: Crea una pull request
pullRequest.beforeCreate: Clicca su **Create pull request** e compila il modulo.

form.title: Puoi inserire qualcosa come "`feat: Translated {file} into {language}.`".
form.message: Lascia un messaggio indicando cosa hai tradotto e cosa resta da tradurre.

done.title: Fatto!
done.message: La tua proposta sarà revisionata e probabilmente approvata se rispetta gli standard di qualità di Open Authenticator 😎

translate.unsavedChanges: Sembra che tu abbia modifiche non salvate. Se lasci questa pagina, tutto andrà perso. Vuoi continuare?
translate.error: Errore durante la richiesta dei dati di traduzione.

error.description: Puoi continuare a navigare tornando alla pagina precedente o andando alla [home page](/).
error.notFound: Se qui dovrebbe esserci qualcosa, [invia un messaggio](/contact).
